topics.name: Living Things and Human Health (JSS1 - JSS3)
topics.description: Living organisms, cellular organization, human body systems, reproductive health, diseases, and drug abuse.

subtopics.name: Living Things and Cell Organization
objectives: Distinguish characteristics of living and non-living things
Identify plant cell and animal cell organelles and their functions
Explain organizational levels of living things from cells to organisms

subtopics.name: Human Body Systems and Health
objectives: Describe digestive, circulatory, respiratory, excretory, and reproductive systems in humans
Explain pubertal changes, personal hygiene, and reproductive health in adolescents
Identify transmission, prevention, and control of communicable diseases (malaria, HIV/AIDS, cholera)

subtopics.name: Drug and Substance Abuse
objectives: Define drug abuse, misuse, addiction, and prescription drugs
Identify social, psychological, and physiological consequences of drug abuse

topics.name: Matter, Chemical Changes, and Energy (JSS1 - JSS3)
topics.description: States of matter, elements, compounds, mixtures, chemical symbols, kinetic theory, energy forms, and forces.

subtopics.name: Matter and Atomic Structure
objectives: Describe solid, liquid, and gas states using Kinetic Theory of Matter
Distinguish elements, compounds, and mixtures with examples
Write chemical symbols and formulas for common elements and compounds

subtopics.name: Chemical Changes and Reactions
objectives: Distinguish physical changes (melting, boiling) and chemical changes (rusting, burning)
Identify acids, bases, and salts using litmus paper and pH indicators

subtopics.name: Energy, Work, and Power
objectives: Identify mechanical, thermal, electrical, chemical, and solar energy forms
Apply law of conservation of energy to energy transformations
Calculate work done (W = F × d) and power (P = W / t)

topics.name: Earth, Space, and Environmental Science (JSS1 - JSS3)
topics.description: Solar system, Earth rotation/revolution, weather, climate, environmental pollution, and natural resources.

subtopics.name: Solar System and Earth Movements
objectives: Identify planets in the solar system, sun, moon, and satellites
Explain rotation of Earth (day and night) and revolution of Earth (seasons)
Describe solar and lunar eclipses

subtopics.name: Environmental Pollution and Protection
objectives: Identify sources and consequences of air, water, and land pollution
Explain deforestation, desertification, soil erosion, and climate change effects
Describe refuse disposal, recycling, and environmental sanitation practices
